notes: Self-managing, self-healing architecture initiative; products-not-just-concept; heterogeneous management across IBM and non-IBM environments; precursor to autonomic computing branding.
Self-managing computing paradigm formalized by IBM shortly after this report; direct lineage to AIOps, site-reliability engineering, and self-healing cloud infrastructure.
Umbrella-messaging companion to eLiza; cited as part of the 'Pervasive + Real-time demands Managed Computing' framing.
Real-time enterprise enabled by compute tools and decision support; just-in-time manufacturing requires end-to-end automated supply chain.
IBM E-server Analyst Symposium (spring 2001) introduced initial eLiza messaging; report distinguishes eLiza as management strategy, not server-specific.
eLiza self-management should be transparent to developers — no new API set required.
Contrasted as low-bar operational innovation versus policy-driven autonomous remediation.
